steps: Power the VTX and connect it to a computer over USB so it mounts as storage.
Copy Avatar_Sky_<version>.img to the root of the VTX storage; do not rename the file, and remove any older .img files first if present.
Safely disconnect USB and keep the VTX powered with adequate cooling.
After the unit finishes booting, press and hold the Link button for about 8 seconds to start the firmware update.
Keep power connected until the LED pattern returns to its normal state.
Confirm the new version in the goggles OSD after reboot.
Copy AvatarMini_Sky_<version>.img to the root of the Mini VTX storage.
Do not rename the file, and remove any older .img files first if present.
Power on the Mini VTX and allow it to finish initial boot.
Press and hold the Link button for about 8 seconds to start the firmware update.
Keep power connected until the LED returns to its normal pattern.
Check the new version in the OSD after reboot.
Connect the Moonlight VTX to a computer using the official Moonlight USB cable so the device storage is accessible.
Copy AvatarMoonlight_Sky_<version>.img to the device storage root.
Do not rename the file, and remove any older .img files first if present.
Power the unit correctly and allow it to finish booting.
Press and hold the bind/link button for about 8 seconds to start the firmware update.
Keep power connected until the LEDs return to their normal boot pattern.
Verify the new version in the goggles OSD after reboot.
Format the SD card in the goggles first.
Copy Avatar_Gnd_<version>.img to the root of the goggles SD card; do not rename the file, and remove any older .img files first if present.
Insert the SD card into the goggles and power them on.
Wait until the goggles reach the standby screen.
Press and hold the red Link button for about 8 seconds to start the firmware update.
Do not power off during the update.
After the upgrade completes and the goggles reboot, enter the settings menu and perform RESET.
Check the new version in the goggles menu.
Copy AvatarX_Gnd_<version>.img to the root of the Goggles X SD card; do not rename the file, and delete any older firmware images first.
Insert the SD card into the Goggles X and power them on.
After initialization, press and hold the Link button for about 8 seconds to start the firmware update.
Keep the goggles powered until the update finishes and normal boot resumes.
Verify the new version in the goggles menu.
CRITICAL: New-batch Goggles X units with the newer DDR memory must not be downgraded below 38.44.13 or they may be permanently bricked.
Copy AvatarLite_Gnd_<version>.img to the root of the Goggles L SD card; do not rename the file, and delete any older .img files first.
Insert the SD card into the goggles and power them on.
Allow initialization to complete.
Press and hold the Link button for about 8 seconds to start the firmware update.
Keep the unit powered until the update completes and the beeping stops.
Verify the new version in the goggles menu after reboot.
Copy AvatarSE_Gnd_<version>.img to the root of the VRX TF/SD card; do not rename the file, and remove any older .img files first if present.
Insert the card into the VRX and power it on.
Press and hold the REC button for about 6 seconds to start the firmware update.
Wait for the update to finish and the unit to reboot normally.
Confirm the firmware version afterward.
Power on the Relay.
Press and hold the VTX frequency binding key for about 8 seconds to enter USB mass-storage flashing mode.
Connect the Relay to a computer over USB.
Copy Avatar_Relay_<version>.img to the device storage root; do not rename the file, and remove any older .img files first if present.
Disconnect USB so the Relay reboots and starts the firmware update automatically.
Wait until the LED returns to its normal success pattern.
Verify the new version via the Relay status output.
